title: Engagement Is a Design Choice
thesis: Engagement is a design choice shaped by business model and metric; the same platforms could choose ranking signals, friction, and business models that reward substance over extraction, but doing so requires changing what success is measured by.

series.title: Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment

claims.claim: Engagement is a design choice, not a natural law; it emerges from the metric a platform chooses to optimize, and that metric can be changed.

evidence.snippet: Milli et al. compare Twitter's engagement-based ranking with a reverse-chronological baseline, showing that the ranking signal itself drives the content users see.
The EU Digital Services Act requires large platforms to offer users a non-personalised recommender-system option, treating the choice of ranking as a regulable design decision.

counterevidence.summary: Platform metrics may be constrained by competitive pressure and user expectations, so changing one platform's metric does not guarantee users will stay if rivals keep optimizing for engagement.

claims.claim: When a platform ranks content by predicted engagement, it systematically favors content that triggers strong emotional reactions, often at the expense of accuracy, civility, and long-term user well-being.

evidence.snippet: The engagement-based algorithm amplified tweets expressing anger, sadness, and anxiety, and significantly increased out-group animosity relative to a chronological timeline.

counterevidence.summary: Some studies on other platforms find that algorithmic feeds can expose users to more ideologically diverse or reliable news than chronological feeds, suggesting effects are platform- and implementation-specific.

claims.claim: Engagement-based ranking algorithms amplify emotionally charged and out-group-hostile content compared with reverse-chronological feeds, and users do not prefer the political content the algorithm selects.

evidence.snippet: Of political tweets selected by Twitter's engagement-based algorithm, 62% expressed anger (vs. 52% chronological) and 46% contained out-group animosity (vs. 38% chronological); users rated political tweets from the algorithm lower on stated preference.

counterevidence.summary: The experiment was conducted on U.S. Twitter users in 2023; cultural and platform differences may alter the magnitude of effects in India.

claims.claim: Likes, shares, and retweets act as reinforcement signals: users learn to produce the content that the platform rewards, and the platform then ranks that content more highly, creating a self-reinforcing feedback loop.

evidence.snippet: Users who received more likes and retweets for outrage-expressing tweets went on to express more outrage over time, especially in politically moderate networks.

counterevidence.summary: The study observes a learning effect, not a universal law; other factors such as offline events, identity, and media diet also shape expression.

claims.claim: The ad-supported attention economy concentrates rewards among top creators and foreign platforms while turning most users' attention into inventory.

evidence.snippet: Meta and Google India together reported gross advertising revenue of roughly ₹57,472 crore (~$6.8 billion) in FY24–25.
Only 8–10% of India's 2–2.5 million active digital creators monetize effectively, while the ecosystem influences over $350 billion in consumer spending.

counterevidence.summary: The ₹57,472 crore figure is derived from separate ROC filings; combined totals vary by reporting convention and do not capture all domestic or informal digital advertising.

claims.claim: Alternative ranking signals, friction, and business models can reduce divisive amplification, but only if platforms are willing to measure success differently.

evidence.snippet: An exploratory ranking by users' stated preferences reduced angry, partisan, and out-group-hostile content relative to the engagement-based timeline.
The EU Digital Services Act requires large platforms to provide a non-personalised feed option, a regulatory recognition that ranking design can be changed.

counterevidence.summary: Stated-preference ranking in the Milli et al. study also reduced exposure to out-group content, raising echo-chamber concerns; trade-offs between engagement, satisfaction, and civic outcomes remain unresolved.

claims.claim: At India's scale, the choice of engagement metric is a public-interest question: the same design shapes national attention, social trust, youth learning, and the creator economy.

evidence.snippet: The Economic Survey 2025–26 flags digital addiction as a public-health challenge with real economic and social costs for India's demographic dividend.
The Kerala High Court restricted personal mobile-phone use during work hours in December 2024, reflecting institutional recognition of attention-design harms.

counterevidence.summary: The public-interest framing is a policy judgment, not an empirical claim; reasonable people can disagree about the right balance between platform autonomy and regulation.

sources.title: Milli et al.: Engagement, User Satisfaction, and the Amplification of Divisive Content on Social Media (PNAS Nexus / Knight First Amendment Institute)
Brady et al.: How social learning amplifies moral outrage expression in online social networks (Science Advances / Yale)
BCG: From Content to Commerce: Mapping India's Creator Economy
IBEF / industry reports: Tech giants' advertising revenue from India
European Commission: The Digital Services Act
LiveLaw: Kerala High Court restricts personal mobile-phone use during work hours

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit and trace every answer to specific evidence in the artifact.
Preserve the evidence-cautious, non-preachy tone; do not present platform design as deterministic or users as passive.
Distinguish U.S. platform experiments from India-specific data; label global findings as structural parallels, not direct Indian measurements.
When citing revenue or creator-economy figures, note that they are derived from industry filings and consulting estimates, not audited national statistics.
Cross-check any regulatory update against the latest MeitY / EU notices before treating it as current.

sectionOutline.title: The Business Model Behind the Metric
